meta.title: Pulmonology Practice Software | Respiratory RTM & PFT Coordination | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to pulmonology practice operations. $9,100/month in respiratory RTM. PFT and sleep study coordination. Oxygen therapy tracking. Biologic authorization for severe asthma.

hero.subhead: Your COPD and asthma patients already track symptoms — that's billable respiratory RTM. Your PFTs and sleep studies need coordination from order to interpretation. Your oxygen patients need ongoing management. clinIQ captures RTM revenue, coordinates testing, and tracks respiratory therapies.

sections.content:
Pulmonology practices manage chronic respiratory disease while coordinating significant testing volume.

**The chronic disease opportunity:**

COPD patients should track: dyspnea, exacerbations, rescue inhaler use, activity tolerance, oxygen needs.

Asthma patients should track: symptom days, rescue inhaler use, peak flow (if applicable), triggers, controller compliance.

ILD and pulmonary fibrosis patients should track: dyspnea progression, oxygen requirements, functional status.

This monitoring is clinically valuable and now billable as respiratory RTM.

**The testing coordination problem:**

Pulmonology orders extensive testing:
- Pulmonary function tests (spirometry, full PFTs, DLCO)
- Sleep studies (HST, PSG)
- CT imaging
- Bronchoscopy
- 6-minute walk tests

Each test requires scheduling, completion, interpretation, and follow-up. Without tracking, tests fall through cracks.

**The oxygen management burden:**

Oxygen patients need ongoing management:
- Initial qualification
- Equipment prescription
- Periodic re-qualification
- Titration adjustments
- CMN renewals

This work is time-consuming and often untracked.

**The math:**

70 COPD/asthma patients on respiratory RTM × $130/month = $9,100/month = $109,200/year.

Plus time savings from testing coordination and oxygen management.

CMS created specific respiratory RTM codes. Your COPD and asthma patients qualify.

**What respiratory patients track:**

COPD:
- Dyspnea level (daily or weekly)
- Exacerbation symptoms
- Rescue inhaler use (puffs per day)
- Activity tolerance
- Oxygen saturation (if monitoring)
- Controller medication compliance

Asthma:
- Symptom days per week
- Nighttime awakenings
- Rescue inhaler use
- Peak flow (if using meter)
- Controller compliance
- Trigger exposure

**The CPT codes:**

98975: RTM setup — one-time. ~$20-25.
98976: RTM device supply (respiratory). ~$55-65/month.
98980: RTM treatment management — first 20 minutes. ~$50-70/month.
98981: Additional 20-minute increments.

Typical: 98976 + 98980 = $120-140/month.

**What clinIQ does for respiratory RTM:**

Disease-specific templates: COPD patients get COPD-appropriate questions. Asthma patients get asthma questions. Each tracks what matters for that condition.

Alert thresholds: Rescue inhaler use increasing? Exacerbation symptoms? Activity tolerance declining? Alerts trigger intervention before hospitalization.

CAT and ACT integration: COPD Assessment Test and Asthma Control Test administered periodically through the app. Trends visible.

Automatic time tracking: Every data review documented toward billing threshold.

**The clinical value:**

Respiratory RTM isn't just billing — it's better care. You see exacerbations developing. You intervene earlier. You prevent hospitalizations.

COPD hospitalizations cost $10,000-30,000. Preventing one hospitalization per quarter pays for the entire RTM program.

Pulmonary function testing is core to pulmonology diagnosis and monitoring:

- Spirometry for COPD/asthma diagnosis and staging
- Full PFTs (lung volumes, DLCO) for ILD, pulmonary fibrosis, pre-op evaluation
- Bronchoprovocation for asthma diagnosis
- Six-minute walk test for functional assessment

**The coordination problem:**

PFT ordered → Is it scheduled? → Was it completed? → Are results interpreted? → Was patient informed? → Is follow-up scheduled?

Each step can fail. The PFT ordered 3 weeks ago might still be unscheduled. The PFT completed last week might sit uninterpreted.

**What clinIQ does for PFT coordination:**

PFT pipeline tracking:
- PFT ordered (date, type)
- PFT scheduled (date, time)
- PFT completed
- PFT interpreted (pending/complete)
- Results communicated
- Follow-up scheduled (if needed)

Aging alerts: 'PFT ordered 10 days ago, not scheduled.' 'PFT completed 5 days ago, not interpreted.'

Interpretation queue: Which PFTs are pending interpretation? Color-coded by age.

Pre-visit integration: When patient arrives, PFT status visible. 'PFT completed 3/20, interpreted 3/22, results in chart.'

**In-house vs referred testing:**

If you do PFTs in-house: Lab availability, tech scheduling, equipment status all trackable.

If you refer out: Referral tracked through external completion and result receipt.

Sleep medicine often lives within pulmonology practices. Sleep study coordination is complex.

**Sleep study types:**

- Home sleep test (HST): Unattended sleep study at home
- Polysomnography (PSG): In-lab attended sleep study
- Split-night: Diagnostic + CPAP titration in same night
- CPAP titration: Titration study after positive diagnostic
- MSLT: Multiple sleep latency test for narcolepsy

Each requires different scheduling, equipment, and interpretation.

**The coordination problem:**

HST ordered → Device shipped → Patient wears device → Device returned → Study scored → Interpreted → Patient informed → CPAP ordered (if positive) → Follow-up scheduled.

Many steps. Many failure points.

**What clinIQ does for sleep management:**

Sleep study pipeline:
- Study ordered (type)
- Scheduled/device shipped
- Completed/device returned
- Scored/interpreted
- Results communicated
- Treatment ordered (if applicable)
- Follow-up scheduled

HST device tracking: Which devices are out? When due back? Who hasn't returned?

CPAP compliance tracking: For practices that follow CPAP patients, compliance data tracking from PAP download.

**Sleep DME coordination:**

After positive sleep study, patients need CPAP equipment:
- DME ordered
- DME delivered
- Setup completed
- Compliance checked (30-day, 90-day)

clinIQ tracks this coordination to ensure patients actually get and use their equipment.

Oxygen patients require ongoing management:

**Initial qualification:**
- Qualifying arterial blood gas or oximetry
- Documentation of condition
- Order written
- CMN (Certificate of Medical Necessity) completed
- DME arranged

**Ongoing management:**
- Periodic re-qualification (typically annually)
- Liter flow adjustments
- Portable vs stationary needs
- CMN renewals
- Problem resolution

**The tracking problem:**

150 oxygen patients. Which ones need CMN renewal? Which need re-qualification? Which haven't had follow-up in 6 months?

Without tracking, management is reactive — you deal with problems when DME companies call.

**What clinIQ does for oxygen tracking:**

Oxygen patient roster: Every oxygen patient, current prescription, qualifying documentation, CMN status.

CMN expiration alerts: 'CMN expires in 30 days. Renewal needed.'

Re-qualification tracking: 'Last qualifying study 11 months ago. Renewal due.'

DME coordination: Order status, delivery status, setup confirmation.

**The compliance value:**

Oxygen documentation requirements are strict. Audits happen. Having organized documentation prevents recoupments and proves medical necessity.

Severe asthma biologics (Nucala, Fasenra, Dupixent, Xolair, Tezspire) require prior authorization.

**The authorization requirements:**

- Documented severe persistent asthma
- Failure of high-dose ICS + LABA
- Specific phenotype documentation (eosinophilic, allergic, etc.)
- Prior biologic trials (for second-line agents)

**What clinIQ does for biologic auth:**

Auth status per patient: Current biologic, auth status, expiration, doses remaining.

Expiration alerts: 'Nucala auth expires in 21 days. Patient due for injection next week.'

Prior medication documentation: ICS + LABA combinations tried, duration, response. Supporting step therapy appeals.

Injection scheduling: For in-office injections, scheduling with auth verification.

**The phenotype documentation:**

Biologics target specific asthma phenotypes:
- Eosinophilic: Requires documented eosinophil counts
- Allergic: Requires documented IgE levels and allergy testing

clinIQ tracks relevant labs to support authorization and phenotype documentation.

Pulmonology implementation covers RTM, testing coordination, and oxygen management.

**Week 1: Configuration**

We configure for your practice: visit types, testing workflows, RTM templates, oxygen tracking.

Respiratory RTM templates: COPD, asthma, ILD.

Testing pipelines: PFT, sleep studies configured for your referral patterns.

**Week 2: Training**

Clinical staff learns RTM workflow. Testing coordinators learn pipelines. Oxygen coordinator learns tracking.

**Week 3: Go-Live**

Testing tracking active. RTM enrollment beginning. Oxygen tracking running.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: Testing coordination immediate. RTM enrollment starting.

Month 3: RTM billing at scale (50-70 patients). Testing turnaround improving. Oxygen management organized.

Month 6: Full RTM revenue ($8,000-10,000/month). Testing coordination smooth. Sleep management optimized.

**ROI Math:**

RTM revenue: 70 patients × $130/month = $9,100/month = $109,200/year

Testing efficiency: Faster PFT turnaround improves patient experience and referring provider satisfaction.

Hospitalization prevention: One prevented COPD admission per quarter = $40,000-120,000/year saved.

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

faqs.q: Which respiratory conditions qualify for RTM?
faqs.a: COPD, asthma, ILD, pulmonary fibrosis, and other chronic respiratory conditions. Any condition where between-visit symptom monitoring informs treatment decisions.

faqs.q: What's the difference between respiratory RTM codes and general RTM?
faqs.a: CPT 98976 is specifically for respiratory system monitoring. It replaces 98977 (general musculoskeletal) when monitoring respiratory conditions. Reimbursement is similar.
Pipeline from order through scheduling, completion, interpretation, communication, and follow-up. Aging alerts surface studies stuck at any stage.
Yes. Devices shipped, devices in use, devices due back, devices returned. No lost devices.
Patient roster with prescription, qualifying documentation, and CMN status. Expiration alerts for CMN renewals. Re-qualification reminders.
Auth status per patient per drug. Expiration alerts. Prior medication trial documentation. Phenotype documentation (eosinophil counts, IgE levels).

faqs.q: Does this integrate with PFT equipment?
faqs.a: We track PFT workflow regardless of equipment. Integration with specific PFT systems varies — contact us about your setup.
If you run pulmonary rehabilitation, session tracking, patient progress, and compliance monitoring can be configured.
